This polymarket trader turned 1000$ into 63000$ on the up or down markets and then stopped trading them after their biggest win

I found another interesting wallet today looking through: https://t.co/7gfTubWDi1 the up or down intel dashboard

This trader deposited just $1.1k in January 2025 last year and started out trading pretty carefully.

They made a few wins, and then took out that initial deposit immediately and traded with the profits.

They then withdrawn more and redeposited almost immediately.

They weren’t hyperactive at first, and honestly, it almost looked like they might’ve been market making or farming rebates for a while because they earned a lot of them.

They made a total of 1200 trades (give or take)

The biggest win is almost their last trade.

Solana Up or Down, bought at $0.01, turned into $12k profit. Basically a 100x.

Bitcoin Up or Down, bought at $0.56, made $4.2k profit. Around 1.5x.

They had a few more like these between 1-1.5x but most are tiny ROI.

They made lots of smaller “safer” bets. Personally, I’m not even sure I’d call those low risk, as you could see with that one big win, at 1 cent it can also go the other way, and they bot lots of “sure” calls at 99 cents.

Still, this wallet ended up as one of the highest-ROI traders in Polymarket Up or Down markets so far to date.

And then they just quit crypto a few days after that big win. No more up or down.

Still trades, but completely different markets, AI Models, Oil, etc, modest wins since then.

So, what do you think happened?

Did he do what most of us promise to but never are able to? Stop at that big win or number?

Full profile: https://t.co/RfTGY2gIgY

Fresh Sports Bettor wins $24.25M in a week: 3 accounts, same funding address

Got notifications about 3 profiles:
• mintblade: $9.24M profit, 5 wins out of 5 bets.
• GRIMDRIP: $7.6M profit, 2 wins out of 2 bets.
• endlessFate: $7.41M profit, 6 wins out of 9 bets.
All three wallets sent their profits to Binance through the same deposit address (0xB08B...317D), suggesting the same person may control them.

Keep in mind, their Polymarket profiles show up weird, not even displaying some of these bets I'm seeing on the Coinpilot app, but the winnings show everything you need to see

After making huge profits, all three wallets stopped trading and withdrew all of their funds.

This goes to show how the biggest and most profitable traders rotate their wallets extremely frequently, on a weekly basis

Maybe possible to track new accounts through where that same address makes more deposits,

Deposit address: https://t.co/lVYo6lrnS0

While the internet argues, the money already voted.

Polymarket's Golden Boot market - $26M in:

🥇 Mbappé - 26%

🥈 Messi - 21% (↑12% this week)

Mbappé says Messi always scores.

The market agrees - Messi just jumped +12% to 21% on Polymarket.

Mbappé still leads at 26%, but he's slipping (−2%).

$26M says the Golden Boot is a two-man duel now. I'm watching Mbappe 's line.

He was down by −$468,576. Three months later - up by $1,800,000. Here’s a breakdown by month 👇

Everyone’s reposting the +$1.8 million. No one’s talking about the 14 months he lost money before that 👇

He started from almost nothing - and made it to $1.8 million. But what really caught my attention was what happened in March

From -$18 to $1,800,000. One Polymarket wallet. 16 months. 📈

Didn't get rich in a straight line. He bled for a year first:

Feb 2025 → -$18 (started from nothing)
Mar 2025 → -$3,802 🔻
Jul 2025 → +$43,762 🔺
Jan 2026 → -$168,707 🔻
Mar 2026 → -$468,576 💀 (almost HALF A MILLION underwater)
Apr 2026 → +$514,339 🚀 (the reversal)
Jun 2026 → +$1,797,504 🏆

He was down nearly $470K and didn't fold. Then flipped it into +$1.8M in under 90 days.

+924.90% all-time. Ranked #28 on Polymarket.
